sanskrit:
नासतो विद्यते भावो नाभावो विद्यते सतः।
उभयोरपि दृष्टोऽन्तस्त्वनयोस्तत्त्वदर्शिभिः॥
transliteration: nāsato vidyate bhāvo nābhāvo vidyate sataḥ | ubhayor api dṛṣṭo 'ntas tv anayoḥ tattva-darśibhiḥ ||
literal_meaning: Of the non-existent there is no coming-into-being; of the existent there is no non-existence. The conclusion regarding these two is seen by the seers of truth.
contextual_meaning: Krishna introduces Arjuna to the distinction between the impermanent (body/mind) and the permanent (Self). By asserting that only the eternal truly exists, he reframes Arjuna’s grief as mis-identification with transient forms.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance: the felt reality of loss collides with the intellectual possibility that nothing real can be destroyed.
trigger_scenario: A patient facing terminal diagnosis realizes every identity label (parent, worker, healthy body) is dissolving and asks, 'If none of this is ultimately real, what am I?'
anchor_text: What truly exists never dies; what dies never truly existed.
